title: calamagrostis paludosa
intro: Calamagrostis paludosa, also known as marsh fescue, is a perennial grass species native to Europe, Asia, and North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Calamagrostis paludosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
